data.description:
## Why
The production-severity axes + mean structurally mis-measure code-review's maintainability/latent-
regression findings: a feature silently dead in prod (untested wiring) scores 0.415 because prod_impact
is 'none' and reversibility 'easy' drag it down. Landmines and nits overlap so no threshold separates them.

## What
A code-review-specific, verifier-embedded impact model dispatched per-gate off `decide.py`:
- Two lanes: prod/correctness (MAX of tier-tagged correctness binaries) x likelihood-of-firing; and
  maintainability (MAX of tier-tagged maintainability binaries) x a change-frequency/centrality floor.
- `impact = MAX(prod, maint)`, then a reversibility det/hybrid FLOOR (hard-to-reverse change escalates
  borderline findings), then a detection amplifier, then a re-anchored [0,1] scale (drop the 0.33 floor;
  a maintainability landmine reaches ~0.8+).
- Each consequence question is a narrow binary tagged with the severity TIER it implies; lane-severity =
  MAX of fired tiers (no dilution; a minor/moderate binary can't alone reach the block zone).
- The audit-approved carved sub-question set with charitable carve-outs. Reversibility/reach/change-
  frequency are DET/hybrid; the LLM sub-answers fold into the SINGLE aggregate Pass-2 verify output
  (no added LLM calls).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (code-review `impact()`), the code-review verify prompt (emit
  the carved tier-tagged sub-answers), det signals, and re-anchoring calibration. Plan-review unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Code-review `impact()` = MAX(prod-lane, maint-lane) with tier-tagged-binary MAX within each lane;
      reversibility floor + detection amplifier + re-anchored scale applied.
- [ ] Serious tier = {data-loss-without-recovery, security-bypass-not-enforced-elsewhere, silent-wrong-
      feeding-a-decision, unversioned-published-contract-break, safety-net-removal-without-replacement},
      each with its charitable carve-out; plus the moderate/minor coverage set.
- [ ] Sub-answers emitted in ONE aggregate Pass-2 call (no added LLM calls); reversibility/reach/change-
      frequency are DET/hybrid; per-gate dispatch (plan-review untouched).
- [ ] Simulation on the labeled/adjudicated set shows the model separates HIGH from NIT better than the
      old scalar (or the gap is explained), validated diff-grounded before any threshold-down tuning.
data.title: Code-review impact model redesign: two-lane tier-tagged MAX with re-anchored scale
